title: 10X Sucrose Orange G Loading Dye Preparation
author: Gordon Wellman
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kp7cvrn

steps.title: Dissolve Sucrose
steps.instruction: Dissolve 10 g (for 25 mL final volume) or 20.0 g (for 50 mL final volume) of sucrose entirely into a smaller volume (e.g., 20 mL or 40 mL respectively) of warm sterile water. Ensure complete dissolution.

steps.title: Add Orange G
steps.instruction: Add 0.05 g (for 25 mL final volume) or 0.1 g (for 50 mL final volume) of Orange G, sodium salt to the sucrose solution.

steps.title: Adjust Volume and Mix
steps.instruction: Top up the solution with sterile water to the final desired volume (25 mL or 50 mL). Mix thoroughly until all components are dissolved. The final solution should be 0.4% (w/v) Orange G and 40% (w/v) Sucrose.

steps.title: Cool Solution
steps.instruction: Allow the loading dye solution to cool completely to room temperature.

steps.title: Aliquot Loading Dye
steps.instruction: Dispense 1 mL aliquots of the prepared loading dye into labelled 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes for convenient future use.

steps.title: Store Loading Dye
steps.instruction: Store the aliquoted loading dye at -20 °C until use. The dye keeps well for years at this temperature.
